message:
style: cargo fmt pass on fuzz targets and time test

Pure formatting sweep, no behavioural change. cargo fmt had accumulated
drift across the fuzz target set because several files were hand-edited
while adding new cases; this restores consistency so `cargo fmt --check`
is clean on the frankenlibc-fuzz crate again and so unrelated feature
commits stop carrying spurious fmt deltas.

Specifically reformats:

- crates/frankenlibc-core/src/time/mod.rs — collapse the multi-line
  difftime assert back into rustfmt's canonical function-argument
  layout with each format-arg on its own line.

- crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_{ctype,inet,locale,
  malloc,math,open_syscalls,printf,regex,resolv,resolver,scanf,
  stdlib}.rs — shorten a handful of assert!/assert_eq! chains whose
  argument lists rustfmt wants on a single line, and normalise use
  import ordering in fuzz_elf_loader.rs (ProgramType/RelocationType/
  SectionType/Elf64Symbol/ElfHashTable group members now follow
  alphabetical order per rustfmt).

No fuzz logic changes; every `.any(...)`, `.matches!(...)`, and
oracle assertion keeps the exact same semantics as before.
